run_isolation.temporary_repo_state: comparison metrics copied back; temporary caches and worktree are not required by committed artifacts

clean_probe_results.label: clean_dspy_3_1_3_gepa_probe
clean_probe_results.notes: The clean run found a strong candidate early, then spent the remaining observed budget exploring malformed typed-output variants. It was interrupted rather than promoted to a committed artifact because GEPA did not finish.

clean_probe_results.label: clean_dspy_3_2_0_baseline
clean_probe_results.notes: The clean DSPy 3.2.0 baseline on the historical model pair already matched the best full-valset score observed during the interrupted DSPy 3.1.3 optimize probe, leaving little honest headroom for a replacement GEPA artifact.

notes: The historical DSPy 3.1.3 artifact remains committed because it is a completed GEPA artifact, while the 2026-04-28 clean rerun was interrupted before compile completion.
The clean DSPy 3.2.0 baseline saturated this small typed-output benchmark enough that forcing a new optimized artifact would be less informative than preserving the historical non-saturated artifact.
Temporary .venv-dspy313/.venv-dspy320 state is not used; all refresh evidence came from isolated uv --with invocations and disposable caches.
